session_focus: AdSense approval fix (PR #176) - Legal pages, GDPR consent, sitemap, and SSR hydration

patterns_found.name: App Shell Component Store Injection
patterns_found.symptoms: Test failures in app.spec.ts when a new store-dependent component is added to app shell
patterns_found.root_cause: All existing app shell tests fail because the new component requires a store mock, but the test module instantiation doesn't provide it

evidence.issue: CookieConsentBannerComponent injection into app shell caused app.spec.ts to fail
evidence.fix_pattern: Add AdsStore provider mock to TestBed setup

patterns_found.impact: Immediate - blocks all tests when new stores are injected into root component

patterns_found.name: Edit Tool Replace String Collisions
patterns_found.symptoms: Unintended string replacements in import paths and token names from replace_all with short search strings
patterns_found.root_cause: Short search strings like 'COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY' match multiple locations beyond the intended target

evidence.solution: Manual verification required; consider more specific replacement patterns

patterns_found.impact: Medium - requires manual fixing after replace operations

patterns_found.name: SSR Hydration: Server vs Client Store State Divergence
patterns_found.symptoms: Store state (e.g., consentAnswered: false) differs between server render and client, causing hydration mismatch
patterns_found.root_cause: Store initialization happens on server with browser APIs unavailable (localStorage). Client doesn't re-sync initial state.

evidence.issue: AdsStore consentAnswered was false on server (no localStorage), never updated on client
evidence.solution: Use withHooks onInit to detect browser environment and update state after SSR hydration

patterns_found.impact: High - silent failures, ads don't render despite user consent

patterns_found.name: Angular Testing Framework Context API Mismatch
patterns_found.symptoms: Test fails with undefined function error (vitest.fn() in Jest context, $localize not defined in Jest)
patterns_found.root_cause: Testing APIs differ between Vitest and Jest; Vitest and Jest have different setup files
evidence: vitest.fn() → use jest.fn() in Jest-based tests
Missing import '@angular/localize/init' in Jest test files
TestBed.overrideProvider after module instantiation causes state leakage

patterns_found.name: Static File Serving: URL Rewrite vs sendFile Trade-offs
patterns_found.symptoms: Choice between Express sendFile (security concerns) and URL rewrite middleware for serving root-level static files
patterns_found.root_cause: sendFile requires careful rate limiting; rewrite + express.static is simpler but requires localized path structure

evidence.issue: CodeQL flagged sendFile without rate limiting for robots.txt
evidence.solution: Express middleware rewrite: /robots.txt → /de/robots.txt, let express.static serve; add Cache-Control: 1h

patterns_found.pattern: For i18n + static files, prefer rewrite middleware + express.static over sendFile

patterns_found.name: Blog hreflang Alternates with Different Language Slugs
patterns_found.symptoms: hreflang alternates point to non-existent routes when DE and EN blog posts have different slugs
patterns_found.root_cause: Blog slugs are language-specific (DE: 'mein-post', EN: 'my-post'), so hreflang alternates for blog routes 404

evidence.issue: Sitemap includes hreflang for /blog/* routes, but EN slug doesn't exist
evidence.solution: Disable hreflang for blog routes; only add for static pages with matching i18n URLs

patterns_found.pattern: Only use hreflang for routes with identical structure in all languages

patterns_found.name: AI Review Comments on German Typography
patterns_found.symptoms: False positive review comments about German quotation marks and dashes being unusual characters
patterns_found.root_cause: AI reviewers may not recognize German typography as intentional localization

evidence.issue: Comments flagging German quotation marks and dashes as non-ASCII characters in XLIFF
evidence.solution: Expected for German locale; can be dismissed

patterns_found.pattern: German i18n will trigger false positives on German typography

skill_adjustments.content:
## Testing Framework Compatibility

When writing tests for projects using different testing frameworks:

### Jest Context (libs, e2e)
- Use `jest.fn()` instead of `vitest.fn()`
- Import `'@angular/localize/init'` in test files that use $localize
- TestBed providers should be set up once per describe block, not overridden after instantiation

### Vitest Context (web app)
- Can use `vitest.fn()`, `vitest.vi.spy()`, etc.
- Locale initialization may differ

### Best Practice
- Check `project.json` under test configuration to determine framework
- For multi-framework projects, maintain separate test setup patterns per framework
skill_adjustments.reasoning: Prevents test setup errors and speeds up debugging when working across Nx projects with different test frameworks
SSR hydration bugs are hard to debug in production. This pattern prevents silent state divergence.

skill_adjustments.section: Mock Dependencies for Root Component Tests
skill_adjustments.reasoning: Prevents cascading test failures when modifying the root component

skill_adjustments.content:
## Edit Tool Safety: Replace String Collisions

### Risk Pattern: Short String Replacements

When using the Edit tool's replace_all feature with short strings, unintended replacements can occur:

```
BAD: Search 'CONSENT_KEY', Replace 'COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY'
     → 'pus_CONSENT_KEY' becomes 'pus_COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY' ✓ (correct)
     → 'OLD_CONSENT_KEY' becomes 'OLD_COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY' ✗ (unintended)

GOOD: Search 'pus_CONSENT_KEY', Replace 'pus_COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY'
      → More specific, fewer collisions
```

### Best Practices
- Always include context (module name, prefix, scope)
- For token names, include the full path from root: 'COOKIE_CONSENT_KEY'
- Test replace on a small file first before large-scale replacement
- Review diff carefully after replace_all operations

### When to Use Manual Fix
- If collision is discovered, manually revert and use more specific pattern
- Don't rely on replace_all for refactoring token names across many files
skill_adjustments.reasoning: Prevents subtle bugs from unintended string replacements that are hard to spot in review

skill_adjustments.content:
## Sitemap & hreflang: Language Slug Considerations

### Pattern: Only Add hreflang for Parallel Routes

hreflang alternates only work when the URL structure is identical across languages:

```
GOOD:
  DE: /de/about → EN: /en/about (parallel structure)
  → hreflang link: <de> links to /de/about, <en> links to /en/about

BAD:
  DE: /de/blog/mein-post → EN: /en/blog/my-post (different slugs)
  → hreflang would link DE post to EN post with different slug (404)
```

### For Content with Different Slugs (Blog Posts)
- Disable hreflang for blog routes
- Allow each language version to rank independently
- Consider: translating slugs to maintain parallel structure (more effort, better SEO)

### Implementation
```typescript
const routes = sitemap.routes
  .filter(r => !r.url.includes('/blog/')); // Exclude blog from hreflang

// Add hreflang only for static pages
if (hasParallelStructure(route)) {
  addHreflang(route);
}
```

### Impact
- Missing hreflang: Search engines may not index both language versions
- Broken hreflang (404): Confuses search engines, hurts ranking
- No hreflang for blog: Each language version ranks independently (acceptable)
skill_adjustments.reasoning: Prevents broken hreflang links that confuse search engines and hurt SEO for localized sites

process_improvements.suggestion: When injecting a store into app root, immediately run app tests and update mocks. Consider: can this store be lazy-loaded instead? Root component dependencies ripple across all tests.
process_improvements.impact: Prevents cascading test failures during subsequent development

process_improvements.suggestion: Before running large replace_all operations, test on a single file or limited scope. Review full diff for unintended replacements.
process_improvements.impact: Catches collision bugs before they spread across codebase

process_improvements.suggestion: When designing state management for SSR apps, explicitly document which signals depend on browser APIs. Mark these with comments and use withHooks onInit for hydration sync.
process_improvements.impact: Prevents silent SSR hydration bugs that are hard to debug in production

process_improvements.suggestion: For ads/monetization features: checklist should include sitemap generation, legal pages (Impressum, Datenschutz), GDPR consent banner, and NPA mode. Auto-generate sitemap via build dependency.
process_improvements.impact: Ensures compliance + SEO requirements are not missed in future monetization work

architectural_patterns.name: SSR Hydration Pattern: Browser State Sync
architectural_patterns.description: For signals that depend on browser APIs (localStorage, window), use withHooks onInit to detect browser environment and update state after hydration
architectural_patterns.success_example: AdsStore now syncs consent state on client after SSR
architectural_patterns.when_to_use: Any store signal that reads from localStorage, sessionStorage, window, or document
architectural_patterns.when_not_to_use: Server-side only state (e.g., Firestore real-time listeners that are already browser-gated)

architectural_patterns.name: Static File Serving Pattern: URL Rewrite vs sendFile
architectural_patterns.description: For i18n + static files at root (robots.txt, sitemap.xml), prefer Express middleware rewrite + express.static over sendFile. Rewrite /robots.txt → /de/robots.txt, let express.static serve with 1h Cache-Control.
architectural_patterns.success_example: robots.txt served at root without security concerns, CodeQL clean
architectural_patterns.when_to_use: Static files that need to be served at root path in i18n apps
architectural_patterns.when_not_to_use: Large files that need streaming (use sendFile with rate limiting)

architectural_patterns.name: Sitemap Multi-Language Pattern
architectural_patterns.description: Generate sitemap with hreflang only for routes with parallel structure across languages. Skip hreflang for routes with language-specific slugs (blog).
architectural_patterns.success_example: Sitemap successfully generated with hreflang for static pages, excluded for blog
architectural_patterns.when_to_use: Multi-language sites with some parallel routes and some content-specific routes

technical_insights.insight: Separate cookie consent (pus_cookie_consent) from analytics consent (pus_analytics_consent). Cookie consent gates ad rendering via consentAnswered signal; analytics consent gates GTM/GA4. AdsStore hydrates consent on browser init via withHooks.
Non-personalized ads (NPA) don't require GDPR opt-in but require a consent notice. Banner alone satisfies this. Personalized ads require explicit consent (consentAnswered + targetedAdsConsent).
For i18n + static files (robots.txt, sitemap.xml), store files in locale folder (/de/robots.txt) and use Express rewrite middleware. Simpler and more secure than sendFile.
technical_insights.source: Express middleware implementation for root file serving

technical_insights.insight: Sitemap generation can be wired as a dependsOn in Nx build configuration, ensuring it runs before web:build. This keeps generated files in sync automatically.
